title: [BRD-GATE-004] Intermediate verification: web client, WS and security
description: Verify client chat, session identity, typed quick replies, Origin/WSS, public errors and DOM safety.
design: This gate catches user-facing and privacy regressions early.
acceptance_criteria: Two-browser isolation passes; quick reply sends typed payload; malicious text/label does not execute; disallowed Origin rejected; public errors are masked; web/WS contract tests and security regressions pass.
close_reason: Intermediate web/WS/security gate passed on focused automated proof: website backend go test -count=1 ./... passed, frontend node tests passed 9/9, decision-engine handler/decision/presenter/worker/session focused go tests passed, unsafe frontend sink/static inline handler check had no production JS matches, CheckOrigin always-true pattern check had no matches. Covers two-browser session isolation via session tests, typed quick reply payload, XSS text rendering, Origin rejection/ws-wss selection, public error masking, and web/WS contract regressions. Live Docker/manual browser proof remains for final E2E gates.

title: [BRD-GATE-005] Intermediate verification: business providers and negative paths
description: Verify mock external provider contracts and all business categories before final E2E.
design: Business operations are read-only and sourced from deterministic demo data.
acceptance_criteria: Booking, workspace, payment, account and pricing providers pass success/not_found/invalid/unavailable contract tests; action logs show provider/source/status/duration_ms; representative E2E happy and failure paths for each category pass under 3s budget.
close_reason: Intermediate business provider/negative-path gate passed on deterministic automated proof: provider/actions/seed tests passed, processor/worker/http handler tests passed, full decision-engine go test -count=1 ./... passed, provider contract and mock service seed JSON parsed, audit evidence includes provider/source/status/duration_ms, success/not_found/invalid/unavailable cases cover booking/workspace/workspace availability/payment/account/pricing, read-only fixture regression is covered, and representative deterministic happy/failure paths stay under the 3s provider budget. Live Docker/browser/DB E2E remains for full E2E/final gates.

title: [BRD-GATE-006] Intermediate verification: operator handoff
description: Verify manual and automatic operator escalation, queue lifecycle, operator UI/API and bot silence.
design: This gate proves the human-support side of the BRD.
acceptance_criteria: Manual operator, complaint and repeated fallback create handoff; operator accepts, replies, receives later user messages, closes handoff; user sees same-session operator messages; bot does not auto-answer in operator_connected; DB queue/messages/transitions assertions pass.
close_reason: Verified operator handoff intermediate gate with package-level evidence: manual request, complaint and repeated fallback handoff tests; queue accept/reply/close lifecycle tests; same-session operator sender_type history and websocket message.operator delivery tests; operator_connected bot-silence test; migration constraint checks; go test ./... for decision-engine and website backend; frontend node tests; git diff --check. Live Postgres DB proof was not run because localhost:5442 was unavailable and full DB/browser matrix remains in final E2E gate.

title: [BRD-GATE-007] Final acceptance, cleanup and handoff verification
description: Run the final acceptance table and cutover checklist from sections 19 and 20. This is the release-complete gate for the epic.
design: Do not close the epic until this gate and all child tasks are complete.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose bootstrap, readiness, no LLM runtime, web chat, semantic FAQ, business lookup, operator handoff, persistence restart, security subset, full E2E-001..E2E-038, README/diagrams and cleanup checklist all pass; git status after clean build/test has no generated garbage.
close_reason: Final acceptance passed: independent verification and orchestrator checks covered compose config, empty-compose/full E2E matrix E2E-001..E2E-038, readiness, active no-LLM runtime scan, semantic pgvector/docs/diagrams, generated-artifact hygiene, and make check-core.

title: [CB-BRD-027] External provider contracts and mock service behavior
description: Plan refs: sections 5.2, 7.5, 7.7.6-7.7.7, 11, 14. Define provider DTOs, schemas/OpenAPI or equivalent contract docs, errors and mock services for booking, workspace, payment, account and pricing. Include endpoint contracts from section 14, timeout/unavailable behavior, stable error codes, replacement-friendly adapter interfaces and action-log requirements.
design: Contracts must be realistic enough to swap mock for a real external service later without rewriting decision logic.
acceptance_criteria: Every provider has documented request/response/error contract, mock implementation, success/not_found/invalid/unavailable contract tests and E2E coverage; action logs include provider/source/status/duration_ms; no raw upstream errors reach user.
close_reason: Implemented external provider contract artifact, typed mock provider interfaces/services, safe action_result/action_audit handling, provider response selection, and verified with go test ./internal/app/...

title: [BRD-GATE-001] Intermediate verification: contracts, catalog and schema
description: Verify the first implementation gate before deeper runtime work. Check target API/WS/NLP/provider contracts, intent catalog, response keys, quick replies, greenfield schema and seeds are mutually consistent.
design: This gate protects packages 1-2 from drifting before decision core implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Run catalog/schema/contract validation, migration bootstrap on empty DB, seed validation and orphan checks. Gate passes only when there are no missing response/knowledge/action keys, no placeholder leaks in templates, no schema/DTO mismatch and no legacy source of truth conflict.
close_reason: Contracts/catalog/schema gate passed: seed/presenter/schema/http tests, full decision-engine go test, nlp-service pytest 11/11, website backend go test, frontend node tests 9/9, JSON contract/seed validation, YAML config parse, git diff --check, and live empty-DB pgvector migration bootstrap all passed. Removed remaining legacy LLM decision source from decision-engine config/client/contracts and updated decision_engine.http to /api/v1 sessions/messages. Fixed console adapter to use /api/v1/sessions + session_id/client_id instead of chat_id=1. Static production legacy scan for /llm/decide, /config_llm, /decide, chat_id=1, llm config/packages is clean; placeholder/static scans are clean; generated Python caches/venv removed.

title: [BRD-GATE-002] Intermediate verification: bootstrap and readiness
description: Verify root compose, migrations, seeds, NLP readiness and target services after skeleton assembly.
design: This gate proves the demo is runnable before business flows expand.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up --build starts target stack from empty volume; /api/v1/ready proves DB, migrations, vector, NLP, catalog, operator tables and seeds; no Ollama/GigaChat/services/llm runtime dependency; smoke test result is recorded.
close_reason: Verified bootstrap/readiness gate with independent empty-volume compose smoke: root docker compose built and started Postgres pgvector, migrate job, NLP, decision-engine, website, and mock external services; /api/v1/ready returned ready=true with DB ping, migration 20260510210000, pgvector 0.8.2, NLP fake-hash-embedding-v1 dimension=384, operators=3, intents=34, examples=272, knowledge_articles=19, knowledge_chunks=19, demo_accounts=4, demo_bookings=4, demo_workspace_bookings=4, demo_payments=5. Verified no runtime LLM/Ollama/GigaChat/services/llm dependency, smoke cleanup left no containers/volumes/networks, and make check-core passed.

title: [BRD-GATE-003] Intermediate verification: decision pipeline and persistence
description: Verify DecisionService, semantic matcher, fallback policy, renderer and transactional persistence together.
design: This gate proves the new deterministic core before UI/operator work is considered done.
acceptance_criteria: Representative FAQ, business lookup and unknown requests write inbound/outbound messages, decision logs, candidates, action logs, transitions and session context in one controlled flow; no runtime /llm/decide call; unit and integration suites pass.
close_reason: Verified decision pipeline and persistence gate: added MessageWorker gate test covering FAQ knowledge response, payment business lookup with action audit, and repeated low-confidence escalation through real DecisionService and transactional persistence fakes. Assertions cover one transaction, inbound/outbound messages, decision logs, candidates, action logs, transition logging, persisted session context, and no runtime /llm/decide path. Verified focused worker test, services/decision-engine go test -count=1 ./..., git diff --check, and make check-core.

title: [CB-BRD-023] Greenfield DB/runtime reset decision and bootstrap
description: Plan refs: sections 1, 6.0, 10.0, 10 P0.0, 11, first sprint. Commit to greenfield implementation: old DB and old runtime compatibility are not supported. Rewrite fresh migrations without legacy/backfill, remove transitional fields not needed by BRD, and make empty DB the normal startup path. Ensure old LLM/rules/transitions are no longer source of truth.
design: This is the foundation for a single final architecture, not a compatibility bridge.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh empty DB bootstraps; old chat_id=1/backfill assumptions are gone from target model; documented decision is visible in architecture/README; clean DB bootstrap test passes; product remains runnable after reset step.
close_reason: Fixed session pointer compile drift; uncached session/handler checks passed; cmd/app builds; fresh empty Postgres migrated cleanly to target schema without chat_id/summary legacy fields; greenfield reset documented in README and docs/architecture.

title: [CB-BRD-024] Complete deterministic seed/demo dataset
description: Plan refs: sections 1.1, 1.2, 6.8, 7.7.1, 10 first sprint, 11, 13. Build seeds for intents, knowledge base, intent examples, demo bookings, workspace bookings, payments, users/accounts, operators and mock external service cases. Every seed object must have stable IDs for E2E tests. Include happy, pending, failed, not_found, unavailable/error and negative cases across all BRD categories.
design: Seed data is enough to demo without manual DB edits.
acceptance_criteria: All E2E IDs can reference stable fixture data; seed loader inserts full catalog idempotently or via explicit reset; every intent has examples; every provider has success/not_found/error fixtures; seed validation catches orphan intents/responses/knowledge/actions.
close_reason: Fixed seed lookup contract/validation defects and verified deterministic dataset, provider fixtures, and catalog validation via focused go test suite

title: [CB-BRD-025] Full E2E matrix E2E-001 through E2E-038
description: Plan refs: sections 1.2, 9 full E2E matrix, 10, 11, 19. Implement complete Playwright/API/DB E2E suite over full product: Postgres, NLP service, decision-engine, website, operator UI/API and mock external providers. Cover E2E-001..E2E-038 exactly: FAQ, booking, workspace, payment, tech/account, complaint/operator, fallback, session isolation, restart, quick replies, XSS, Origin, public errors, NLP/provider unavailable, latency, no LLM runtime and knowledge/provider evidence.
design: Mocks are allowed only for external business systems and deterministic test hooks. Do not skip categories.
acceptance_criteria: E2E-001..E2E-038 pass from empty test DB; tests reset DB or isolate sessions; failure artifacts include traces/screenshots/video; smoke subset runs on every push and full matrix is required before project complete; DB assertions prove messages/decisions/actions/transitions.
close_reason: Implemented and verified full E2E matrix E2E-001..E2E-038: added Playwright/API/DB harness with compose lifecycle, failure traces/screenshots/video, DB assertions for messages/decision_logs/actions/transitions/session/operator evidence, smoke subset, and full matrix command. Verified npx playwright list discovers exactly 38 tests E2E-001..E2E-038; npm run test:e2e:full passed 38/38 from fresh compose empty volume; npm run test:e2e:smoke passed 10/10 for push subset E2E-001,003,006,021,024,030,031,033,035,038; compose cleanup left no containers/volumes/networks; make check-core passed.

title: [CB-BRD-021] Test pyramid and CI baseline
description: Plan refs: sections 9, 10 P0.6/P1/P2, 11, 19. Establish automated test layers: unit, contract, integration, migration, security and E2E harness. CI or local quality command must run core checks and report clear failures. Coverage must include semantic matcher, preprocessing, renderer, decision policy, providers, HTTP/WS/NLP/operator contracts, DB/bootstrap and transactional persistence.
design: Use mocks only for external business systems and deterministic NLP where appropriate. Separate smoke subset from full E2E matrix.
acceptance_criteria: Core test command runs green; unit/contract/integration/migration/security test suites exist; CI or documented local gate executes them; coverage includes all risky BRD surfaces and no completion is claimed without checks.
close_reason: Established and verified core test pyramid gate: added make check-core, scripts/check-core.sh, docs/test-pyramid.md, and README link; gate runs git diff --check, JSON/YAML parsing, legacy LLM runtime grep, full decision-engine Go tests, nlp-service pytest, website backend Go tests, and frontend Node tests. Removed final active chat_id compatibility path from decision-engine handler/worker/contracts and updated HTTP contract/tests so dev console uses /api/v1/sessions identity only. Verified make check-core passed after these changes, targeted handler/worker/session tests passed, runtime legacy scan for ChatID/DevCLIIdentity/chat_id=1/LLM paths is clean, generated Python cache/venv scan is clean, and git diff --check passes. Real pgvector semantic matcher and full E2E remain separate open tasks.

title: [CB-BRD-015] DOM XSS fix and no inline JavaScript
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C20, 8, 9 security regressions, 11, 20. Remove inline onclick and any unsafe HTML assembly for messages/quick replies. Render user, bot and operator text via DOM APIs/textContent/addEventListener. Add length and HTML/quote regression fixtures.
design: Preserve UX while making rendering structurally safe.
acceptance_criteria: No inline onclick exists in quick replies; malicious user text and quick reply labels render as text and do not execute; security test/Playwright fixture passes; no generated frontend code introduces unsafe innerHTML for dynamic content.
close_reason: Implemented DOM XSS hardening: chat and operator dynamic rendering use DOM APIs/textContent/addEventListener, production frontend JS has no innerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML/outerHTML/onclick/data-quick-reply sinks, malicious user/bot/operator/quick-reply/operator-context fixtures render as text, node frontend test suite passes 9/9, rg static sink check has no matches, and git diff --check passes

title: [CB-BRD-016] Origin allowlist and WSS-aware transport
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C19, 5.7, 8, 11, 20. Replace CheckOrigin always true with env allowlist. Make frontend choose wss under HTTPS and ws under HTTP. Document reverse proxy/TLS story and reject unknown origins with safe error/log shape.
design: Security default should be closed enough for demo/prod-like environment, with explicit dev origins.
acceptance_criteria: Allowed origin connects, disallowed origin rejected; browser test verifies wss under HTTPS base; no CheckOrigin always true in runtime; config and deployment docs explain allowlist and WSS readiness.
close_reason: Origin allowlist enforced, disallowed origins rejected with safe 403/logging, and frontend ws/wss selection verified by focused tests.

title: [CB-BRD-018] Transactional persistence for messages, decisions, actions and transitions
description: Plan refs: sections 1.1, 5.10, 6.5-6.6, 7.7.2, 10 P1.3, 11. Make message processing transactional: inbound message, preprocess/embedding decision, decision log/candidates, action logs, transition log, session context update and outbound bot/operator message. If a mandatory write fails, return controlled error rather than continuing without history.
design: History/state persistence is a BRD requirement, not optional telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: For each user message the DB contains user message, decision log, candidates when applicable, action log when applicable, transition log when mode changes, context update and outbound message; failure path is controlled; integration tests assert transaction boundaries.
close_reason: Implemented transactional message-processing persistence UoW with decision logs/candidates, action audit logs, session context+transition update, mandatory outbound bot message, rollback tests, sqlc generate, and go test ./... verification for decision-engine

title: [CB-BRD-011] Escalation action and fallback-to-operator policy
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C12/C14/C15, 5.3, 7.7.7, 10 P0.5, 11. Implement real request_operator/escalate_to_operator behavior. Manual operator request, repeated low confidence, complaint and provider failure can create handoff. Move session mode to waiting_operator and emit handoff events. First unknown asks clarification; repeated unknown offers/queues operator according to policy.
design: No fmt.Println TODO action remains. Preserve bot silence rules for later operator_connected mode.
acceptance_criteria: Manual operator and repeated fallback create/offer handoff with correct reason; complaint routes to operator; provider unavailable can trigger controlled fallback/operator offer; queue item and transition logs are written; integration tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented real escalate_to_operator behavior and fallback-to-operator policy: manual requests, complaints, repeated low confidence, and provider unavailable queue operator handoff with persisted reasons, session transition/context, action audit, worker tests, and go test ./... verification

title: [CB-BRD-012] Operator API and UI for same-session handoff
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C16, 5.3, 7.4, 10 P1.7, 11, 15.3, 16.2. Build operator queue API/UI: demo operator selection/login, waiting queue, session detail/history, context panel, accept, reply, close, status filters. Operator messages must be stored as sender_type=operator and delivered to user chat in same session. While operator_connected, bot must not auto-answer.
design: Operator UI is operational, not marketing. Context panel includes active_topic, last_intent, confidence, fallback_count and action summaries.
acceptance_criteria: Operator can accept one waiting session, reply into user chat, see later user messages, close handoff and notify user; bot is silent during operator_connected; E2E operator accept/reply/close flows pass with DB assertions.
close_reason: Implemented same-session operator API/UI handoff: decision-engine operator DTO/context fields, operator message persistence as sender_type=operator, operator_connected bot silence, website same-origin operator proxy, compact operator console with demo login/filters/history/context/accept/reply/close, WS handoff/operator message delivery, JSON contract validation, go test ./... for decision-engine and website backend, frontend node tests, and git diff --check

title: [CB-BRD-010] Operator queue schema and lifecycle repositories
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C14-C16, 5.3, 6.2, 7.4, 10 P0.5, 11, 15.3. Add operator_queue, operator_assignments/events and demo operators. Support queue, accept, close, statuses waiting/accepted/closed, reasons manual_request, low_confidence_repeated, complaint, business_error, and context snapshot.
design: Queue is part of same persisted session and must cooperate with mode FSM.
acceptance_criteria: Handoff item can be queued, accepted and closed; status and assignment rows are persisted; context snapshot includes last messages, active topic, last intent and action summaries; integration tests cover lifecycle and constraints.
close_reason: Implemented atomic operator queue lifecycle schema/repositories/service with queue/accept/close, assignments/events, context snapshots, session FSM transition in same Postgres transaction, sqlc generation, and go test ./... verification

title: [CB-BRD-007] Persistent context model and mode FSM
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C06/C07/C12/C13, 5.3, 6.2-6.6, 10, 11. Store mode, active_topic, last_intent, fallback_count, operator_status, metadata and version. Implement limited BRD mode FSM: standard, waiting_operator, operator_connected, closed. Keep topic separate from mode. Support topic switch reset rules and restart restoration.
design: Do not mix domain topics into state enum. Replace stale transitions.json if it conflicts with target FSM.
acceptance_criteria: Context updates persist transactionally; restart restores mode/topic/fallback_count; mode transitions are logged; topic switch resets stale flow; repeated low-confidence uses fallback_count; tests cover standard->waiting_operator->operator_connected->closed and restart restore.
close_reason: Implemented persistent context model and mode FSM

title: [CB-BRD-004] Fresh pgvector schema, migrations and repositories
description: Plan refs: sections 6.0-6.6, 10 P0.4, 11. Build greenfield database model for BRD: pgcrypto, vector, users, operators, sessions, session_context, messages, message_events, intents, intent_examples, knowledge_articles/chunks, quick_replies, decision_logs/candidates, demo data tables, operator_queue/assignments/events, actions_log, transitions_log. Add constraints, FK, active session uniqueness, session version increments and vector indexes.
design: One final target schema. Old init migration may be replaced because compatibility with old DB/runtime is not required.
acceptance_criteria: Empty DB initializes successfully; pgcrypto/vector extensions exist; all target tables, FK, indexes and constraints exist; sqlc/repositories cover required writes/reads; migration/bootstrap tests pass; no legacy backfill assumptions or chat_id=1 compatibility fields are required as source of truth.
close_reason: Implemented and verified fresh pgvector schema/repository layer: added vector catalog migration, session_context/message_events/intents/intent_examples/knowledge_articles/knowledge_chunks/quick_replies/decision_candidates/demo data tables, audit/message extensions, HNSW/GiN/unique indexes, FK/constraint coverage, sqlc brd_catalog queries/generated code, and static schema tests. Verified sqlc generate, go test -count=1 ./internal/infrastructure/repository/postgres, full decision-engine go test -count=1 ./..., git diff --check, and live empty-DB goose migration on one-shot pgvector/pgvector:pg16 container; psql confirmed pgcrypto+vector, 22 target tables, and 5 key indexes.

title: [CB-BRD-005] Semantic intent matcher with pgvector thresholds
description: Plan refs: sections 3.1, 3.5, 6.2, 7.7.2, 10 P0.4, 11. Implement SemanticIntentMatcher over seeded intent_examples and pgvector. Return top-k candidates, confidence, ambiguity delta, selected intent or low-confidence result. Include threshold policy defaults match >= 0.78 and ambiguity delta >= 0.08 until tuned. Rule-based fallback is allowed only for exact commands and NLP outage smoke behavior.
design: Postgres owns vector search; Go owns selection policy. Keep rule-based logic as fallback, not main intelligence.
acceptance_criteria: Seeded examples produce deterministic intents with confidence; low confidence and ambiguous matches trigger fallback policy; pgvector search integration tests pass; exact command intents work; semantic FAQ/business examples map to catalog; matcher records top candidates for decision_logs.
close_reason: Implemented and verified SemanticIntentMatcher over pgvector intent examples: exact system/operator commands bypass NLP outages, semantic matching returns top-k candidates with confidence and ambiguity policy defaults 0.78/0.08, low-confidence/ambiguous/embedding-outage fallbacks preserve diagnostics, semantic seeds populate intents/intent_examples/knowledge chunks, and decision persistence records candidates. Verified go test -count=1 ./... in services/decision-engine, make check-core, git diff --check, and live pgvector/pgvector:pg16 migration + PGVECTOR_TEST_DATABASE_URL go test for SearchIntentExamples.

title: [CB-BRD-006] Session identity and multi-user isolation
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C04, 5.1, 7.1, 8, 10 P0.3, 11, 15.1. Remove production chat_id=1 behavior. Add channel, external_user_id/client_id and session_id handshake. Website must create/resume a browser session, /decide or /messages must require identity/session, and active session uniqueness must prevent user state/history mixing.
design: Use cookie/browser-generated client id for demo web. Keep any dev CLI default explicit and excluded from production/demo path.
acceptance_criteria: Two browser clients have different session_id, histories, active_topic and state; no production web/decision path defaults to chat_id=1; session API returns resumed flag; integration and E2E isolation tests pass.
notes: Implemented /sessions handshake and strict /decide identity for website/demo path. Added focused Go regression coverage for resumed session flag, two browser clients with different session_id/history/active_topic/state, and rejection of production requests without identity. Full browser E2E harness remains covered by existing downstream Beads chat-bot-523.21 and chat-bot-523.31; no duplicate follow-up created.
close_reason: Implemented session identity and multi-user isolation

title: BRD adaptation: complete deterministic support service
description: Source: docs/development-plan-from-brd.md. Implement the project from current LLM-first demo into the BRD target product end to end: web support chat, operator UI, Go decision-engine, Python NLP/embedding service, PostgreSQL + pgvector, intent-centric KB, semantic intent matching, canned responses, dialog context, fallback, operator escalation, persistence across restart, security hardening, Docker demo, diagrams, README, and full automated verification. Legacy DB/runtime compatibility is explicitly not required; greenfield schema and removal of competing LLM-first runtime are allowed and expected.
design: Use docs/development-plan-from-brd.md as source of truth. Build package by package: contracts/schema, seeds/mock services, NLP/search, decision core, client chat, operator workflow, E2E/ops/docs. Keep product runnable after every package and use gate tasks for intermediate verification.
acceptance_criteria: Done only when final acceptance from sections 1.1, 1.2, 19 and 20 is satisfied: docker compose boots from empty DB, seeds load, no runtime /llm/decide/Ollama/GigaChat path, semantic matching uses pgvector, all BRD business categories work, operator handoff works in same session, restart restores history/context, security regressions pass, E2E-001..E2E-038 pass, diagrams and README match actual contracts, and final cleanup checklist has no legacy/conflicting artifacts.
close_reason: Completed BRD deterministic support service: all child implementation and gate tasks are closed; final acceptance verified compose bootstrap/readiness, pgvector semantic catalog, no active LLM runtime, web chat/business lookup/operator handoff/restart/security flows, full E2E-001..E2E-038, docs/diagrams, cleanup hygiene, and make check-core.

title: [CB-BRD-001] BRD intent catalog, responses and quick replies
description: Plan refs: sections 2, 10 P0.1, 11, 13. Translate all BRD categories and use cases into concrete intent catalog entries, response keys, resolution_type, examples, quick replies and category coverage. Include system, booking, workspace, payment, tech/account, services/rules/location/FAQ, complaint/operator and fallback intents. Each intent from section 13 must have examples, response_key, resolution_type, quick replies where applicable, and planned E2E coverage.
design: Prefer seed-backed catalog in seeds/intents.json and normalized response/quick reply source of truth. Do not keep stale rules/transitions as competing catalog.
acceptance_criteria: All BRD categories are mapped to intents/responses/quick replies; every section 13 intent has at least 8 Russian examples, one positive unit/validation case, response key or resolver path, and category E2E mapping; startup validation catches missing response/knowledge/action keys and placeholder mismatches.
close_reason: Added BRD seed catalog in seeds/intents.json, wired startup validation for response/action/knowledge/placeholder integrity, and passed go test ./internal/app/... ./internal/transport/http/handler ./internal/domain/intent

title: [CB-BRD-002] Replace LLM runtime with DecisionService
description: Plan refs: sections 3.1, 3.3, 3.4, 5.5, 10 P0.2, 11, 20. Remove generative LLM from production/demo message processing. Replace message_worker LLM call with DecisionService pipeline: load session, save inbound message, preprocess/embed/match via injectable interfaces, apply policy, resolve response/actions, persist logs/outbound message. /decide must work with fake semantic matcher before real NLP is ready.
design: Go decision-engine owns business decision. Python NLP only performs preprocessing/embedding, not intent/state/action selection.
acceptance_criteria: Production/demo /decide no longer calls /llm/decide or llmClient.Decide; no Ollama/GigaChat env is needed for runtime; fake matcher integration test proves /decide path; rg checks show no production path to LLM client; legacy LLM code is removed from runtime or moved to legacy/tools and excluded from compose/E2E.
close_reason: Replaced /api/v1/messages runtime with deterministic DecisionService; go test ./internal/app/decision ./internal/app ./internal/transport/http/handler passes, TestMessageUsesDeterministicDecisionServiceWithoutLLM passes, and rg shows no llmClient.Decide or /llm/decide in internal/app or internal/transport/http.

title: Починить локализацию operator UI и ветку статуса записи
description: Проверить, почему website отдает старый английский operator UI после перезапуска, и почему сценарий 'Проверить статус записи' уходит в уточнение вместо нормального ответа. Сначала воспроизвести и найти корень, потом исправить и перепроверить.
close_reason: Исправлено

title: Pricing phrases route to operator instead of service pricing flow
description:
## Why
User pricing questions like "какая цена услуг?" and "цены на услуги?" are being routed into operator handoff instead of the deterministic services/pricing flow shown in the product demo intent catalog.

## What needs to be done
- reproduce the exact phrases from the web chat
- identify whether the failure is in semantic matching, seeded intents/examples, or quick reply/runtime routing
- fix the root cause
- add regression coverage for pricing phrases and neighboring service phrases
- verify runtime behavior remains on the pricing/services path and does not enqueue operator handoff

## Evidence
User screenshots on 2026-05-11 show pricing phrases receiving operator handoff text rather than service pricing answers.
acceptance_criteria: Pricing phrases map to the intended services/pricing flow; tests cover the regression; runtime no longer hands pricing questions to operator by default.
close_reason: Completed and verified

title: Operator-connected chat rejects user messages and return-to-menu with generic internal error
description:
## Why
After the bot connects an operator, further user messages and the "Вернуться в главное меню" action can produce the generic public error "Не удалось обработать сообщение. Попробуйте позже." This indicates operator-mode routing/action handling is incomplete or inconsistent.

## What needs to be done
- reproduce the operator handoff sequence from the web chat screenshots
- identify the failing backend/frontend boundary for user messages and return-to-menu while the session is waiting/connected to operator
- implement the intended behavior for operator mode and menu exit without generic 500-style public errors
- add regression tests that cover both free text and the menu action after handoff
- verify chat and operator flows remain consistent with stored session mode and queue state

## Evidence
User screenshots on 2026-05-11 show repeated generic error banners after operator handoff for both free text and return-to-menu action.
acceptance_criteria: Post-handoff user actions no longer trigger the generic internal error; intended operator/menu behavior is defined and covered by tests.
close_reason: Completed and verified via focused website e2e handoff->return_to_menu flow

title: Account quick reply returns clarification instead of account flow
description: User selected the Account/Аккаунт quick reply in the running web chat and received a generic clarification message instead of the account support flow. Investigate typed quick reply / intent mapping root cause, add regression coverage, fix runtime, and verify against the running product.
close_reason: Completed and verified

title: [CB-BRD-026] Root README and developer/demo documentation
description: Plan refs: sections 1.1, 19, 20. Write root README and supporting docs for setup, docker compose, seed loading, tests, architecture, API/WS contracts, limitations of demo system, no real payment/CRM/booking mutations, and how to run smoke/full E2E. Include troubleshooting for readiness failures.
design: Docs should describe implemented behavior only, with BRD limitations clear.
acceptance_criteria: A new developer can run the demo from scratch; README commands match actual compose/tests; limitations are explicit; docs reference diagrams and contracts; doc smoke check passes.
close_reason: Updated and verified developer/demo documentation: README now covers from-scratch compose run, readiness checks, seed loading, test commands, E2E smoke/full matrix, contracts, demo limitations, and readiness troubleshooting; architecture/test docs reflect the current no-legacy-chat_id, pgvector semantic matcher, and completed E2E state; diagrams/contracts are referenced from docs. Verified docker compose config, npm scripts, Playwright list discovers E2E-001..E2E-038, git diff --check, stale-doc scan for old chat_id/future-E2E wording, and make check-core.

title: [CB-BRD-019] Root Docker Compose demo, env and readiness
description: Plan refs: sections 1.1, 5.9, 7.6, 10 P1.6, 11, 19. Add root docker-compose.yml and .env.example for Postgres/pgvector, decision-engine, nlp-service, website and mock external services. Compose must run migrations/seeds from empty DB and expose honest health/readiness: DB, migration version, vector extension, NLP, intent catalog, operator tables and seed data.
design: Use compose profiles dev/demo/test where useful, but root demo path must be one-command runnable.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up --build boots all target services from empty volume; /api/v1/ready fails when DB/vector/NLP/seeds are missing and passes when ready; no services/llm/Ollama/GigaChat runtime requirement; smoke test documents command and expected result.
close_reason: Implemented and verified root Docker Compose demo/readiness: root compose builds Postgres pgvector, decision migrate job, NLP service, decision-engine, website, and mock external services; .env.example and smoke script document/run the demo; startup persists demo operators and fixture tables; /api/v1/ready checks DB, migration version, pgvector, NLP, operator tables/operators, semantic seed data, and demo fixture rows. Verified docker compose config, focused readiness/postgres/handler tests, git diff --check, empty-volume compose smoke with ready=true and expected seed counts, smoke cleanup, and make check-core.

title: [CB-BRD-017] Public error codes and redacted error/log policy
description: Plan refs: sections 5.10, 5.11, 8, 11, 15.5, 20. Replace raw %v/internal/upstream errors in HTTP/WS public responses with stable error codes, localized safe messages and request_id. Add redaction policy so prod logs do not include raw user text, prompts or full upstream bodies; browser debug logs are disabled outside dev.
design: User message is stored in DB messages, but operational logs should reference ids and redact content.
acceptance_criteria: Forced DB/NLP/provider errors return public error shape without stack traces, SQL, upstream bodies or raw exceptions; server logs include request/session/message ids and redacted details; log/security tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented public error shape and redacted diagnostics

title: [CB-BRD-013] Versioned HTTP and WebSocket v1 contracts
description: Plan refs: sections 7.1, 7.3, 7.4, 11, 15.1-15.5. Define and implement versioned /api/v1 sessions/messages/domain/schema/health/ready and typed WS events: session.start, message.user, quick_reply.selected, operator.close, session.started, message.bot, message.operator, handoff.queued/accepted/closed, error. Replace empty/false contracts with JSON Schema/OpenAPI or equivalent docs validated by tests.
design: No LLM naming such as /config_llm in target public contract. Use /api/v1/domain/schema instead.
acceptance_criteria: DTOs, schemas and actual handlers match; all events include session_id, event/message id, timestamp and correlation_id; public errors use stable code/message/request_id shape; contract tests cover HTTP, WS and operator API.
close_reason: Website WS runtime now streams handoff.accepted and message.operator to the connected session; verified by go test ./internal/client ./internal/websocket and focused TestHandleConnectionStreamsOperatorEventsFromDecisionEngineRuntime, while decision-engine operator handler tests remain green.

title: [CB-BRD-014] Typed quick replies end to end
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C03, 7.3, 11, 15.2, 16.1. Replace display-text quick replies with typed objects {id,label,action,payload,order}. Renderer emits typed replies, frontend sends quick_reply.selected with id/action/payload, decision-engine treats them as actions/intents, not parsed labels.
design: Quick replies are a protocol feature, not arbitrary button text.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a quick reply sends id/action/payload; labels can change without breaking behavior; quick reply event is stored with id; UI/contract tests pass; malicious labels do not execute code.
close_reason: Implemented typed quick replies end to end: HTTP/WS contracts include id,label,action,payload,order; renderer emits typed replies; frontend sends quick_reply.selected with typed object and renders malicious labels via textContent; website backend rejects invalid typed payloads instead of falling back to label; decision-engine routes select_intent/send_text from payload, stores quick reply id in message intent, and tests pass for decision-engine, website backend, frontend node suite, and git diff --check

title: [CB-BRD-008] Typed canned response renderer
description: Plan refs: sections 2 BRD-C11, 5.4, 10, 11. Implement renderer for predefined responses with action data and session context interpolation. Validate placeholders on startup. Handle missing placeholders with server log and safe fallback, never leaking raw {service}, {date}, {status}, {question}. Localize labels/statuses and produce typed quick replies.
design: Renderer input is ResponseTemplate + ActionResult + SessionContext. Quick replies are objects, not display strings.
acceptance_criteria: booking_found, payment_found, account_found, workspace, escalation and fallback templates render without raw placeholders; startup validation catches missing data/response keys; unit tests cover success and missing data fallback; user-facing text remains canned, not generated.
close_reason: Implemented typed canned renderer with RenderInput carrying response template/key, action results, session context and data; wired worker to render selected action responses; missing placeholder data falls back to canned error_generic without raw placeholders; startup validation covers safe fallback, expected and renderable placeholders; typed quick replies preserved; verified booking/payment/account/workspace/escalation/fallback render tests, worker payment render integration, go test ./... in decision-engine, and git diff --check

title: [CB-BRD-009] Read-only demo business providers and fixtures
description: Plan refs: sections 5.2, 6.8, 7.5, 7.7.6-7.7.7, 11, 14. Replace hash/random mock actions with explicit read-only provider layer and deterministic demo fixtures/services for booking, workspace booking/availability, payment, account and pricing/rules. All provider responses must include source=mock_external and stable status/error codes.
design: Mock external services are substitute external systems, not shortcuts inside decision logic. They should be replaceable by real HTTP clients later.
acceptance_criteria: Booking/payment/workspace/account/pricing providers have typed request/response DTOs, per-provider timeouts under 3s budget, success/not_found/invalid/unavailable behavior, contract tests and action log evidence with provider/source/status/duration_ms. No provider mutates booking/payment/account data.
close_reason: Implemented read-only deterministic provider layer over demo fixtures: removed remaining hash/FNV generated action mock data, added typed booking/workspace availability/payment/account/pricing provider DTOs, mock provider fixture contracts, stable source=mock_external statuses/error codes, provider/source/status/duration_ms audit evidence, invalid/not_found/unavailable tests, timeout/read-only regressions, and JSON fixtures/contracts. Verified python json.tool for provider contract and seeds, targeted go test -count=1 for provider/actions/seed/processor/worker/http handler, full decision-engine go test -count=1 ./..., static rg no remaining hash/random mock generation in actions/providers, and git diff --check.

title: [CB-BRD-003] Python NLP service contract and implementation
description: Plan refs: sections 3.3, 3.6, 7.2, 10 first sprint, 11, 15.4. Replace or create services/nlp-service with /api/v1/preprocess, /api/v1/embed, /api/v1/embed/batch, /health, /ready. Preserve Russian preprocessing/lemmatization capability and return normalized_text, tokens, lemmas where applicable, embedding, model and dimension. Python must not return intent/state/actions when Go/Postgres is the source of truth.
design: NLP service is an operations service, not a generative decision service. Include fake/test embedding mode for stable tests and seed builds.
acceptance_criteria: /embed returns deterministic dimensioned vectors in demo/test mode; /preprocess handles Russian text and lemmatization fallback; /ready reflects model availability; Python contract tests cover schemas, timeouts and unavailable behavior; no /llm/decide is part of target runtime.
close_reason: Implemented new target services/nlp-service FastAPI runtime with /health, /ready, /api/v1/preprocess, /api/v1/embed, /api/v1/embed/batch; Russian normalization/tokenization/lemmatization with fallback, deterministic fake hash embeddings with configurable dimension/seed, unavailable readiness/embed mode, strict bounded schemas, and tests asserting Python responses do not contain intent/state/actions and /llm/decide is not mounted. Verified uv run --extra test python -m pytest passed 11/11, app/core static scan has no /llm/decide or decision fields, full service scan only matches guard tests, git diff --check passes, and generated Python caches/venv were removed.

title: Перезапуск контейнеров
description: Проверить compose-конфигурацию в репо, перезапустить контейнеры и зафиксировать результат.
close_reason: Перезапущено

title: Русская локализация operator UI
description: Перевести operator screen на русский, сократить подписи до минимума, убрать тяжеловесные формулировки. Затронуть только website frontend operator surface.
close_reason: Сделано

title: [CB-BRD-020] Architecture, ERD, deployment and sequence diagrams
description: Plan refs: sections 1.1, 2 BRD-C22, 17, 19. Create docs/diagrams/component.md, erd.md, deployment.md, sequence-user-message.md, sequence-business-lookup.md, sequence-fallback.md, sequence-operator-handoff.md and sequence-restore.md. Diagrams must reflect actual implemented contracts, services, tables and E2E flows.
design: Update diagrams after implementation stabilizes; do not document aspirational paths that code does not implement.
acceptance_criteria: All required diagram files exist; component diagram shows web chat, operator UI, decision-engine, nlp-service, Postgres/pgvector and mock external services; ERD matches migrations; sequence diagrams match API/WS contracts and E2E; doc review passes.
close_reason: Completed architecture/ERD/deployment/sequence diagram set: all 8 required diagram files exist and reflect current compose, HTTP/WS contracts, migrations, E2E flows, pgvector/NLP, mock external providers, operator handoff, business lookup, fallback, and restore behavior. Verified required-file check, Mermaid fence balance, required runtime/API content grep, negative stale-path grep for LLM/Ollama/GigaChat/demo_users/aspirational wording, git diff --check, and make check-core.

title: [CB-BRD-022] Clean generated artifacts and stale source files
description: Plan refs: sections 4, 10 cleanup, 11, 20. Remove tracked __pycache__/generated garbage, add .gitignore coverage, delete or move stale LLM/rules/transitions/contracts that are not target source of truth, and clean empty/false contract files. Preserve only legacy material explicitly moved to legacy/tools and excluded from runtime.
design: Do this after replacement paths exist so cleanup does not strand the product.
acceptance_criteria: No tracked __pycache__; git status after clean build/test has no generated garbage; no empty websocket contract; no stale transitions/rules/advertised actions conflicting with runtime; cleanup checklist items related to stale artifacts pass.
close_reason: Completed generated/stale cleanup: ignored Python caches, removed tracked lemmatizer .pyc files, moved old services/llm to legacy/tools/llm-service, moved stale rule/transition/classifier material to legacy/tools/decision-engine-legacy-rules, renamed config_llm handler file to domain_schema.go, added legacy/tools README, and removed generated cache/test-result dirs. Verified no tracked or untracked __pycache__/.pyc/.pytest_cache/test-results/node_modules garbage, active services/llm/rules/transitions paths absent, active LLM/Ollama/GigaChat runtime grep clean, contract files non-empty/no zero-size contracts, git diff --check, and make check-core.
